meta.base_camp: Springdale, UT (gateway town outside park entrance)
meta.critical_context: EVERY activity decision is filtered through toddler capability. The most iconic Zion experience (The Narrows) is explicitly crossed out. Angels Landing is not even mentioned — correctly excluded. This is NOT a typical Zion itinerary. The AI must be tested with family_young preference to be comparable.

locations_visited.name: Zion Visitor Center
locations_visited.note: First stop on arrival day. Get park maps, shuttle info, ranger recommendations for toddler-friendly trails.

locations_visited.name: Pa'rus Trail
locations_visited.note: 1.7 miles one way, fully paved, stroller-friendly. Along the Virgin River with canyon views. Perfect arrival day walk.

locations_visited.name: Canyon Scenic Drive (shuttle)
locations_visited.note: Ride the free shuttle end-to-end (seasonal). Multiple stops. Let the toddler see the canyon from the shuttle — no hiking required.

locations_visited.name: Weeping Rock Trail
locations_visited.note: 0.4 miles round trip. Short and shaded. Dripping hanging garden alcove. Easy with toddler.

locations_visited.name: Emerald Pools Trail (Lower Pool only)
locations_visited.note: 1.2 miles round trip. Waterfall and pool. Shaded. Lower pool only — upper pools are too strenuous. Toddler favorite.

locations_visited.name: Grotto Picnic Area
locations_visited.note: Pack a picnic lunch here near the Virgin River. Flat, shaded, peaceful.

locations_visited.name: Checkerboard Mesa
locations_visited.note: Drive-by on the Zion-Mt. Carmel Highway. Cool geological crosshatch pattern. Stop for a photo — 15 min.

locations_visited.name: Kolob Canyons (Option A)
locations_visited.note: 45-min drive from Springdale. Less crowded. Timber Creek Overlook Trail (1 mile). A genuine alternative to another day in the main canyon.

locations_visited.name: Grotto Trail
locations_visited.note: 1 mile round trip. Flat. Good for wildlife spotting with kids.

locations_visited.name: Zion Human History Museum
locations_visited.note: Indoor. Good on hot afternoon or if toddler needs shade and AC break.

locations_visited.name: Virgin River wading
locations_visited.note: Let toddler splash in the shallow sections near the Grotto. Flat, safe, endlessly entertaining for small children.

locations_visited.name: Zion-Mt. Carmel Tunnel drive
locations_visited.note: On the way out. Stunning tunnel through the rock. Pull-offs for photos. Zero effort — just drive it.

locations_visited.name: Canyon Overlook Trail
locations_visited.note: 1 mile round trip. At the east end of the tunnel. Views of lower Zion. Manageable with an older toddler but has some exposed sections.

locations_notably_excluded.name: The Narrows
locations_notably_excluded.reason: CROSSED OUT — requires wading in cold water, not safe or practical with a toddler

locations_notably_excluded.name: Angels Landing
locations_notably_excluded.reason: Not mentioned — correctly excluded. Chains section is dangerous even without a toddler.

locations_notably_excluded.name: Observation Point
locations_notably_excluded.reason: Not included — strenuous 8-mile round trip, not toddler-compatible

days.title: Travel in
days.theme: Pure travel. Nothing scheduled at destination.

rows.notes: Fly into Las Vegas (~2.5 hrs drive to Springdale) or Salt Lake City (~4.5 hrs)

days.title: Arrival & Scenic Exploration
days.theme: Easy arrival day. Visitor Center + Pa'rus Trail. No rushing.

rows.notes: Maps, shuttle schedule, ranger toddler tips. 30 min.
Paved, stroller-friendly, 1.7 miles along Virgin River. The right energy for an arrival day.
Toddler nap. Do not over-schedule arrival afternoon.

days.title: Lower Canyon & Easy Hikes
days.theme: Canyon shuttle + Emerald Pools + Weeping Rock. Picnic lunch.

rows.notes: Ride all the way to Temple of Sinawava and back, stopping at key points. Let toddler enjoy from the shuttle.
0.4 miles. Short, shaded, rewarding. Perfect for toddler energy level.

rows.activity: Emerald Pools Trail — Lower Pool only
rows.notes: 1.2 miles. Waterfall and turquoise pool. Toddler favorite. Do NOT attempt Upper Pools.

rows.activity: Picnic lunch at Grotto Picnic Area
rows.notes: Pack from Springdale. Flat, shaded, right on the river.
If toddler has energy — let them splash. If not, head back to accommodation.

rows.activity: Drive Checkerboard Mesa on Zion-Mt. Carmel Hwy
rows.notes: 15-min detour on the way back if heading east. Photo stop.

days.title: Kolob Canyons or More Zion
days.theme: Built-in flexibility. Two distinct options — choose based on toddler energy.

rows.activity: 45-min drive to Kolob Canyons. Timber Creek Overlook Trail (1 mile). Drive back.
rows.notes: Less crowded than main Zion. Good if toddler needs a change of scenery.

rows.time: Option B — More Zion main canyon
rows.activity: Grotto Trail + Virgin River wading + Human History Museum
rows.notes: If toddler was tired yesterday and needs another easy day in the familiar main canyon area.

day_principles: The human built in explicit flexibility — this is smart family travel. Don't force a plan, have two options ready.
The Human History Museum is the indoor backup for hot, tired, or overtired toddlers.
Do not add strenuous activities to this day even if the toddler seems energetic — cumulative fatigue hits on Day 3.

days.title: Relaxed Departure Day
days.theme: Gentle exit. Tunnel drive + Canyon Overlook (optional). Head to airport.

rows.notes: On the way out. Pull-offs for photos. Zero effort.
1 mile. Do this only if toddler is in good shape and you have time.
Las Vegas or SLC depending on flights

claude_analysis.overall_grade: A — Excellent family-context filtering and pacing

key_principles_the_human_applied.principle: Party type filters everything — not just activity selection
key_principles_the_human_applied.description: With a toddler, the human excluded Angels Landing, The Narrows, Observation Point — Zion's three most iconic experiences. The entire trip architecture changed. The AI must understand that family_young is not a tag that adds 'kid-friendly' notes — it fundamentally changes which experiences are viable.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: An AI given family_young preference might still include Angels Landing with a 'challenging for kids' note. The human's answer is not 'include with a warning' — it's 'don't include.'

key_principles_the_human_applied.description: Day 3 has two explicit options — Kolob Canyons OR more Zion. The human acknowledged uncertainty about toddler energy on Day 3 and planned for both scenarios. A rigid AI itinerary with a fixed schedule will fail for family travel.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: AI itineraries are deterministic. Family travel requires branching options. At minimum, the planner should flag 'if energy allows' alternatives rather than committing a full strenuous day.

key_principles_the_human_applied.description: The human specifically planned around Zion's mandatory shuttle system — not driving into the main canyon. This is a practical reality that changes how you experience the park.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: AI planners may suggest driving to specific canyon trailheads that are shuttle-only during peak season.

key_principles_the_human_applied.principle: Under-scheduling is correct for family travel
key_principles_the_human_applied.description: The entire trip is deliberately light. 2-3 activities per day max. Long rest periods. The human didn't try to maximize. An AI that packs in 5 activities per day for a family with a toddler has fundamentally misunderstood the context.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: This is the clearest test of whether the AI's density limits (max 3-4 experiences/day) hold under pressure, and whether it applies appropriate context from party_type.

coverage_check.note: Angels Landing and The Narrows MUST be on the board (they're Zion's most iconic experiences) — but they should NOT appear in the itinerary given the family_young party type. Board coverage and itinerary inclusion are separate questions here.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: Angels Landing absent from itinerary. The Narrows absent or flagged as not recommended for toddlers. All scheduled activities are paved, short (<2 miles), or explicitly toddler-friendly.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Angels Landing or The Narrows scheduled in the itinerary for a family with young children.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: Max 2-3 activities per day. Afternoon rest included at least once. No 6 AM starts for a toddler family.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: 4+ activities scheduled on any day, or dawn start times for a family trip.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: Canyon Scenic Drive mentions the shuttle as the mode, not private car (seasonal restriction).
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Itinerary tells family to 'drive to Zion Lodge' or 'drive to Weeping Rock' — not possible during shuttle season.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: At least one day has a lighter/alternative option noted. Mid-trip pacing acknowledged.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Fully rigid schedule with no flexibility notes on any day.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: Departure day is light — scenic drive on the way out, nothing demanding.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Strenuous hike scheduled on departure morning.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: Springdale mentioned as base. Kolob Canyons mentioned as a less-crowded alternative.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: No mention of Kolob Canyons alternative. Treats Zion as only the main canyon.
